name: Älvsbyn (Sweden)

colors.symbolism: the white field on which the arms are placed
the azure field of the municipal shield
gold (Or) — the sun, the triple-mount, and the wavy base

description: A white field bearing the coat of arms of Älvsbyn Municipality: an azure shield charged with a golden sun above a golden triple-mount that spans the shield, issuant from a wavy base.
history: The municipal arms were composed by the Swedish State Heraldry Office (Riksheraldikerämbetet) and established for Älvsby köping on 17 June 1948, then re-registered for Älvsbyn Municipality (formed 1971) in 1974. Blazon: azure, a throughout triple-mount issuant from a wavy base and, above it, a sun, all Or. The sun is an old alchemical symbol for gold, alluding to a legendary gold deposit noted in the region on Olaus Magnus's 16th-century Carta Marina; the triple-mount and wavy base stand for the district's mountains and its river (Piteälven). Älvsbyn has no separately adopted municipal flag; the flag places the municipal arms on a white field.

features.symbolism: the white field of the flag

features.role: the arms of Älvsbyn Municipality — an azure shield with a golden sun above a golden triple-mount issuant from a wavy base
features.symbolism: the sun stands for gold (a legendary local gold deposit), the triple-mount for the district's mountains, and the wavy base for its river
